## Highlights

- **Crash fix вЂ” wrong-passphrase identity import** вЂ” `.rmid` archive import with a wrong passphrase (or a corrupt file) now surfaces "Import failed (wrong passphrase or corrupt archive)" instead of crashing the app with SIGABRT. Caused by a Kotlin/Native bridge gap on `suspend` functions that throw without `@Throws`; swept the rest of the engine's Swift-callable suspend surface (identity reset, manual-contact add, QR card apply, propagation sync, reactions) to prevent the same shape recurring.
- **Defensive cold-start + network-aware reconnect** вЂ” TCP auto-reconnect is deferred off `init()` to a first-frame `.task` and gated on `NWPathMonitor` reporting a satisfied path, so a stalled `getaddrinfo` against the saved hostname can't trip the iOS launch watchdog. When the network drops (Wi-Fi off, airplane mode, walking out of coverage) the UI now flips to Disconnected instead of showing a stale "Connected"; when it comes back, the saved transport reconnects automatically.
- **Inline-tappable Nomad-browser links** вЂ” `[label\`url]` micron links are now tappable inline (Compose-style native link spans via `AttributedString.link` + an `OpenURLAction` interceptor). The duplicate "в†і label в†’ target" rows beneath each paragraph are gone вЂ” the inline link IS the link. Long-press inside the page still selects text, short tap follows the link.
- **"LXMF delivery" no longer surfaces as a contact name** вЂ” the Messages list previously used the service-type label as a fallback name when an LXMF peer announced without a display name, making distinct unnamed peers all look identical. Now falls through to the destination's short-hash so they stay distinguishable. Nodes tab still shows the service-type label where it's useful.
- **Empty bubbles filtered** вЂ” some peers ship zero-body LXMF for reasons we don't decode into a meaningful payload; the conversation list no longer renders them as blank bubbles. The engine still records `MessageReceived` in the diagnostics log.
- **RRC Rejoin escape hatch** вЂ” when the engine's on-Welcome auto-rejoin fails (or the hub drops a membership without us noticing), the room row now offers a Rejoin action in an overflow menu beside Leave, and the room chat view has a Rejoin toolbar button. Re-issues a JOIN to the hub idempotently without dropping local state.
- **Reticulum protocol fixes** (shared with android-v1.2.7) вЂ” three SPEC В§10 Resource sender fixes: never bundle parts into an exhausted `RESOURCE_REQ` window, bound the window to the sender's serve guard, and a retransmit watchdog for lost `RESOURCE_REQ` / part / HMU.
- **K/N build fix** вЂ” `kotlin.concurrent.Volatile` import in `LinkResourceReceiver.kt` so the iosArm64 target compiles after the Resource watchdog change.
